title: WorkingMemory
description: The pinned goal-state block for a roleplay/interview session. Two tiers: an immutable `charter` (written ONLY by the goal authority — a static template here, the world model in full roleplay) and a mutable `state` (the progress estimate the executive rewrites). The summarizing executive may NEVER write `charter`. See docs/specs/2026-06-23-interview-roleplay.md.

charter.description: Committed goal/intention. Written once by the goal authority (template) ⇒ frozen for interview. Executive can never write this.

question_target.description: ACP A4 (RV-M4/RV-M7) — the fixed number of questions an interview drives before wrapping (a preset default, e.g. 5). OPTIONAL + additive: an older charter without it is still valid, and the consumer applies its own default. The control layer server-enforces the wrap via the anchor when question_count reaches this.

state.description: Progress estimate. Mutable; the executive rewrites it (safe-when-wrong). `covered` is monotonic/append-only; `remaining` is derived (charter.checklist - state.covered), never stored.

question_count.description: ACP A4 (RV-M5) — interviewer questions asked so far, computed deterministically at anchor-render from the session message_count (NOT written by the executive). Drives the wrap gate against charter.question_target. Optional (absent for non-interview).

wrap.description: ACP A4 (RV-M5) — the server's wrap signal: true when question_count >= question_target OR elapsed_min >= time_budget_min. The anchor injects a 'close the interview now' directive; this is the server-side enforcement of the 5-question / time-budget structure.

rubric.description: Optional debrief-rubric SIDECAR that rides the seed (NOT written by the executive; NOT in the knowledge block). The goal authority (roleplay `charter::freeze`) attaches it from the script; chat-service `/evaluate` reads it from the seed (`seed.rubric`) to score the debrief. Shape is flexible (e.g. {"dimensions": [...]}). Added at ACP A0.3 — RW-8 surfaced that `freeze()` emits this top-level field for interview presets while the schema forbade it via additionalProperties:false.
